req.title: Create patient (consent)
req.statement: A registered clinician shall create a patient resource with validated demographics; creation with missing mandatory elements shall be rejected. Consent state at the time of the operation must be tested, including post-revocation timing windows.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-101 demands coverage of: (1) successful patient creation with all mandatory demographics by an authenticated clinician (positive/EP), (2) rejection of a request missing a mandatory element such as 'name' with a 422 response (negative/NEG), (3) a boundary case exercising the exact minimum set of mandatory fields—one field present vs. one absent—to probe the validation boundary (BVA), (4) a state-transition case covering consent active→revoked→re-attempt to verify post-revocation timing window rejection. The violation was test[3] having kind=negative with heuristic=STC; STC (State Transition Coverage) requires kind=boundary because it probes a state boundary rather than a pure negative equivalence class. Fixed by changing kind to 'boundary' for that test. (5) a decision-table case combining multiple missing mandatory fields simultaneously to confirm all rule combinations are enforced (DT). Fixtures provide an authenticated clinician token and a base URL; helpers abstract HTTP calls to stay within the 40-line code limit.

tests.name: test_create_patient_all_mandatory_fields_returns_201
preconditions: FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
Patient with identifier 'EP-POS-001' does not already exist
actions: Build a Patient payload containing all mandatory fields: resourceType, identifier, name (family+given), gender, birthDate
POST payload to /Patient with clinician Authorization header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains the submitted family name
trace: R-HLT-101: patient resource stored on valid creation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_patient_all_mandatory_fields_returns_201(
    clinician_auth_header, base_url, unique_identifier
):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "identifier": [{"system": "urn:test", "value": unique_identifier("EP-POS")}],
        "name": [{"family": "Doe", "given": ["Jane"]}],
        "gender": "female",
        "birthDate": "1990-06-15"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Patient",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_header
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Patient"
    assert body["name"][0]["family"] == "Doe"
    assert "id" in body

tests.name: test_create_patient_missing_name_returns_422
preconditions: FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Build a Patient payload that omits the mandatory 'name' field
POST payload to /Patient with clinician Authorization header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome or error message referencing 'name'
trace: R-HLT-101: creation with missing mandatory elements shall be rejected
R-HLT-101: error pathway=422 when mandatory element missing
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_patient_missing_name_returns_422(
    clinician_auth_header, base_url, unique_identifier
):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "identifier": [{"system": "urn:test", "value": unique_identifier("NEG-NAME")}],
        "gender": "male",
        "birthDate": "1985-03-22"
        # 'name' intentionally omitted
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Patient",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_header
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    issue_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "name" in issue_text or "mandatory" in issue_text or "required" in issue_text

tests.name: test_create_patient_exactly_minimum_mandatory_fields_accepted
preconditions: FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
The minimum mandatory field set is: resourceType, name, gender, birthDate
actions: Build a Patient payload containing exactly the minimum mandatory fields (no optional fields)
POST payload to /Patient with clinician Authorization header
Remove one mandatory field (birthDate) and POST again
Capture the second HTTP response status
trace: R-HLT-101: boundary between valid minimum payload and one-field-short invalid payload
R-HLT-101: error pathway=422 when mandatory element missing
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_patient_exactly_minimum_mandatory_fields_accepted(
    clinician_auth_header, base_url, unique_identifier
):
    min_payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "name": [{"family": "Min", "given": ["Test"]}],
        "gender": "unknown",
        "birthDate": "2000-01-01"
    }
    resp_ok = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Patient",
        json=min_payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_header
    )
    assert resp_ok.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp_ok.status_code}: {resp_ok.text}"

    below_min = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "name": [{"family": "Min", "given": ["Test"]}],
        "gender": "unknown"
        # 'birthDate' removed: one field below minimum
    }
    resp_fail = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Patient",
        json=below_min,
        headers=clinician_auth_header
    )
    assert resp_fail.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp_fail.status_code}: {resp_fail.text}"

tests.name: test_create_patient_after_consent_revocation_rejected
preconditions: FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
A consent resource exists for the test subject in state ACTIVE
The system enforces consent checks on patient creation
actions: POST to /Consent/{consent_id} to revoke (set status=inactive) the subject's consent
Immediately POST a valid Patient payload referencing the revoked-consent subject
Capture HTTP response status and body to confirm rejection within the post-revocation timing window
expected: Subsequent Patient creation POST returns HTTP 403 or 422 indicating consent not active
Response body references consent or authorization denial
trace: R-HLT-101: consent state at time of operation must be tested
R-HLT-101: post-revocation timing windows must be covered
R-HLT-101: state transition ACTIVE->INACTIVE enforced at creation boundary
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
import time

def test_create_patient_after_consent_revocation_rejected(
    clinician_auth_header, base_url, active_consent_id, unique_identifier
):
    revoke_payload = {"status": "inactive"}
    rev_resp = requests.patch(
        f"{base_url}/Consent/{active_consent_id}",
        json=revoke_payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_header
    )
    assert rev_resp.status_code in (200, 204), f"Revoke failed: {rev_resp.text}"

    time.sleep(0.5)  # post-revocation timing window

    patient_payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "name": [{"family": "Revoked", "given": ["Subject"]}],
        "gender": "female",
        "birthDate": "1975-11-30",
        "extension": [{
            "url": "urn:consent-ref",
            "valueString": active_consent_id
        }]
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Patient",
        json=patient_payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_header
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (403, 422), f"Expected 403/422 after revocation, got {resp.status_code}"
    body_text = str(resp.json()).lower()
    assert "consent" in body_text or "authorization" in body_text or "forbidden" in body_text

tests.name: test_create_patient_multiple_missing_mandatory_fields_returns_422
preconditions: FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
Mandatory fields are: resourceType, name, gender, birthDate
actions: Build three Patient payloads each missing a different combination of mandatory fields: (a) missing name+birthDate, (b) missing gender+birthDate, (c) missing name+gender+birthDate
POST each payload to /Patient with clinician Authorization header
Capture HTTP response status for each request
expected: All three POST requests return HTTP 422
Each response body references the missing field(s)
trace: R-HLT-101: creation with missing mandatory elements shall be rejected
R-HLT-101: error pathway=422 when mandatory element missing
R-HLT-101: decision-table coverage of multi-field missing combinations
